topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The string quartet was playing a particularly saccharine waltz when I saw him. Standing near the champagne fountain, laughing with a woman whose smile was blinding, was Mark. My stomach clenched instantly, a cold, tight knot forming beneath my ribs. I froze mid-sip of my lukewarm prosecco, the glass trembling slightly in my hand. Every muscle in my body seemed to lock up. I felt a sudden, desperate urge to melt into the expensive floral arrangement beside me. When his eyes swept across the room and landed on mine, a wave of sheer panic washed over me. I managed a strangled sound, a pathetic little cough, and quickly turned my attention to the meticulously arranged centerpiece, desperately trying to become invisible.

[story 2]

"Oh, look, it's Sarah!" My mutual friend chirped, nudging me forward. I followed his gaze, and there he was, leaning against a pillar, looking annoyingly composed. The air seemed to evacuate my lungs. My vision tunneled slightly, the vibrant colors of the ballroom suddenly muted and distant. I could feel a flush creeping up my neck, hot and undeniable. I gripped my cocktail glass so hard my knuckles turned white. Every instinct screamed at me to flee, to somehow dissolve into the polished marble floor. I mumbled something incoherent to the waiter, my voice thin and reedy, focusing intensely on the pattern of the carpet, anything but the sight of him.

[story 3]

I was halfway through a toast, trying to muster a pleasant expression for the assembled guests, when the familiar silhouette appeared. It was him. Standing there, looking impossibly well-dressed and utterly carefree, talking animatedly to someone I vaguely recognized. A strange, heavy pressure settled behind my eyes. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I felt a distinct sensation of wanting to physically recoil, to become smaller, less substantial. I dropped my wine glass—it didn't shatter, thankfully, but the sudden clatter was deafening in my own ears. I quickly bent down, pretending to adjust my dress, my hands shaking so badly I could barely manage the movement.
